news.summary: In Bangalore, India, Neuralix is enhancing the Indian Army's Surya Drishti program through AI technology, improving situational awareness for operational efficiency, safety, and decision-making in the field.
news.headline: Neuralix AI enhances Indian Army's Surya Drishti with AI for situational awareness, improving mission efficiency and safety.

The event demonstrated active shareholder participation and engagement with the company's happenings.
news.headline: Dundee Precious Metals reports annual meeting voting results, conducted online, on May 7, 2025. Characters: 92

news.summary: Allied Gold Corporation (TSX: AAUC) (OTCQX: AAUCF) announced its production results for the first quarter of 2025. During this period, the company produced a total of 84,040 ounces of gold. The total cost of sales, cash costs, and All-in Sustaining Costs (AISC) per ounce sold were reported at $1,838. These figures provide insight into the financial performance and operational efficiency of Allied Gold Corporation in the beginning of 2025.
news.headline: Allied Gold reports strong Q1 2025 results: 84,040 gold ounces produced at $1,838 cost per ounce sold. Optimizing growth projects.

news.summary: On May 8, 2025, Synthetic Grass Warehouse (SGW) will be launching a new distribution center in Houston, TX, providing a convenient location for residents to access high-quality artificial turf products. This expansion is part of SGW's efforts to cater to the landscaping needs of the local community. With the unpredictable weather in Texas, the durable and versatile nature of these synthetic grass products will offer residents a low-maintenance and long-lasting solution for their outdoor spaces.
news.headline: SGW opens new facility in Houston, TX on May 8, 2025, offering durable synthetic turf products for landscaping needs.

news.summary: Innergex Renewable Energy Inc. has agreed to be acquired by CDPQ for $13.75 per share. The acquisition was approved by 99.86% of common shareholders at a meeting. They also achieved commercial operation of the Hale Kuawehi solar facility. Financial highlights for Q1 2025 show an increase in Adjusted EBITDA to $181.9 million. Cash flow from operating activities was $67.9 million. Free Cash Flow for the trailing twelve months was $216.4 million, with Free Cash Flow per share at $1.07. Dividends on common shares amounted to 34% of Free Cash Flow. The corporation is focusing on regulatory approvals for the acquisition and ongoing development projects.
news.headline: Innergex reported Q1 2025 results, including $13.75/share acquisition by CDPQ, reaching commercial operation for solar facility in Hawaii, with $181.9 million in adjusted EBITDA, $1.07 Free Cash Flow per share.

news.summary: The board of directors of The Brink’s Company, based in Richmond, Virginia, announced on May 7, 2025, that they have approved a 5% increase in the regular quarterly dividend on their common stock. The dividend amount has been raised to $0.255 per share. Shareholders of record as of May 19, 2025, will receive the dividend, which is scheduled to be paid on June 2, 2025.
news.headline: Brink’s Company raises quarterly dividend by 5% to $0.255 per share. Payable on June 2, 2025 to shareholders on record May 19.

news.summary: In the midst of economic instability worldwide, the Philippines is emerging as a hotspot for affluent investors. Recognized for its stunning landscapes and inviting culture, the country is attracting attention for its appealing financial benefits including tax incentives, straightforward residency options, and an upscale real estate sector that is currently underrated compared to other global markets. Noteworthy is Manila's remarkable performance in 2023, leading the globe in luxury property price escalation with a significant 26.3% surge.
news.headline: Manila emerges as a global wealth hub with 26.3% luxury price growth in 2023. Business Wire report.

news.summary: On May 7, 2025, in Munich, Envision Energy revealed the release of the EN 8 Pro 8MWh DC Liquid-Cooled Energy Storage System at Smarter E Europe 2025. This cutting-edge technology aims to drive the shift towards a smarter and more resilient energy landscape. Envision Energy, a prominent player in eco-friendly solutions, introduced this system to address the growing demand for efficient energy storage. The EN 8 Pro offers a storage capacity of 8 megawatt-hours and leverages liquid-cooling technology for enhanced performance. This innovation represents a significant step towards sustainable energy solutions and signifies Envision Energy's commitment to advancing green technology on a global scale.
news.headline: Envision Energy launches EN 8 Pro 8MWh DC Liquid-Cooled Energy Storage System at Smarter E Europe 2025.

news.summary: On May 7, 2025, in Seattle, Melissa Shields was named the new executive vice president overseeing Communications, Human Resources, and Organizational Development. She brings over 15 years of experience from Casey Family Programs, where she previously served as the managing director of Human Resources. This appointment reflects Shields' long-standing dedication and expertise in the organization, marking a significant milestone in her career.
news.headline: Melissa Shields named EVP for Communications, HR, and Org Development at Casey Family Programs after 15 years at the organization.

news.summary: On May 7, 2025, Sprott Inc. ("Sprott") disclosed the outcomes of its Annual Meeting of shareholders. All proposals outlined in the Management Information Circular from March 18, 2025, were approved during the meeting. Sprott operates under the ticker symbols NYSE/TSX: SII, and the event was held in Toronto.
news.headline: Sprott Inc. reports successful Annual Meeting, all resolutions in Management Circular approved on May 7, 2025.

news.summary: Exciting progress is expected in the treatment of MPS I, with ongoing research leading to innovative therapies. Promising treatments like stem cell gene therapy, IDUA Gene therapy, and Recombinant DNA (including OTL-203, Lepunafusp alfa (JR-171), RGX-111, and Iduronicrin) are spearheading these advancements. These developments signify a potential breakthrough in improving the management of MPS I, offering hope for patients and healthcare providers alike.
news.headline: MPS I market to grow at 8% CAGR from 2025-2034. Advancements in treatments like stem cell gene therapy and IDUA Gene therapy.

news.summary: Surge Energy Inc. reported strong financial and operating results for Q1/25, with production averaging 23,567 boepd, exceeding the budgeted estimate of 22,500 boepd. Adjusted funds flow increased to $80.1 million, up 5% from Q4/24. Cash flow from operating activities grew by 29% to $83.5 million. Surge distributed $13.0 million in dividends and returned $5.0 million to shareholders through share buybacks in Q1/25. Net debt decreased by 17% to $246.0 million. Surge drilled 24 wells in its core areas, focusing primarily on the Sparky and SE Saskatchewan regions. Financial highlights include a 4% increase in oil sales to $157.2 million, and adjusted funds flow increasing by 28% to $80.1 million in Q1/25. Operating netback per boe rose by 10% to $43.08.
news.headline: Surge Energy Inc. reports Q1/25 with production averaging 23,567 boepd and AFF of $80.1 million, representing a 5% increase.

news.summary: Killam Apartment REIT reported its financial results for the first quarter of 2025, with a growth in funds from operations (FFO) generating $0.28 per unit, a 7.7% increase from Q1-2024. The company achieved a 7.8% same property net operating income (NOI) growth and 6.6% increase in same property revenue. The same property occupancy levels remained at 97.5%. Killam's net income for Q1-2025 was $101.9 million compared to $127.2 million in Q1-2024. The company recorded fair value gains on investment properties of $70.2 million in Q1-2025. Debt metrics showed improvement with debt-to-total assets ratio at a record-low of 39.9%, and debt-to-normalized EBITDA at 9.66x. Killam is aiming to meet its disposition target of $100 - $150 million in 2025 by redeploying sales proceeds towards value creation through their development program and debt reduction. In summary, the quarter showed growth in FFO and AFFO per unit, strong same property NOI growth, and improvements in debt metrics.
news.headline: Killam Apartment REIT reports $0.28 FFO per unit, up 7.7% from Q1-2024, with $59.0M NOI, $101.9M income in Q1-2025. Debt-to-assets at a record low of 39.9%. Sale target of $100-$150M in 2025.

news.summary: ShaMaran Petroleum Corp. released its financial results for Q1 2025, showing significant growth compared to Q1 2024. The company repaid $56 million of its corporate bond, with a total outstanding amount of $173.1 million. Revenue for Q1 2025 was $35.9 million, gross margin on oil sales was $12.5 million, free cash flow before debt service was $37.8 million, and adjusted EBITDAX was $24.5 million. The company had cash of $79.3 million and gross debt of $188.7 million as of March 31, 2025. Average daily oil production also increased. The closure of the Iraq-Türkiye pipeline since 2023 continues to impact operations. The company is working towards a lasting solution for pipeline reopening.
news.headline: ShaMaran Petroleum Corp. reports Q1 2025 results: $35.9M revenue, $12.5M gross margin, $37.8M free cash flow before debt service, $24.5M EBITDAX. Net debt at $109.4M as of March 31, 2025. Pipeline closure impacting operations. Advisory: For full story, visit Benzinga.com.

news.summary: A recent report reveals that the unemployment rate in the United States has dropped to 4.2%, the lowest it has been in over a year. This decrease indicates a positive trend in the job market, with 674,000 jobs being added in the last month alone. The strong job growth is also reflected in the labor force participation rate, which has increased to 62.8%. Additionally, wages have seen a modest increase of 0.4% in the same period. These statistics point towards a steady improvement in the economic landscape, providing hope for both job seekers and the overall workforce.
news.headline: NewLake Capital Partners releases Q1 2025 financial results.

news.summary: In Panama City on May 7, 2025, Copa Holdings S.A. (NYSE: CPA) revealed its financial performance for the first quarter of 2025 (1Q25). The highlights of the report indicate significant developments in the company's operations.
news.headline: Copa Holdings (CPA) releases 1Q25 financial results with key highlights.

news.summary: Lyn Alden argues that a weaker US dollar is necessary for stabilizing the financial system, with Bitcoin and gold poised to benefit from de-dollarization. The US Dollar Index has dropped by 11% since 2025, now at levels from April 2022. The US holds about $102 trillion in dollar-denominated debt with only $5.8 trillion in base money. The US plays a significant role in the global financial system, but a weaker dollar and a shift away from monetary hegemony may be necessary. Bitcoin and the US Dollar (DXY) have an inverse correlation, with a weakening dollar making BTC more attractive. Nations and entities are increasing Bitcoin exposure as the dollar's dominance wanes, signaling a move towards de-dollarization. Countries are exploring alternatives to the dollar for trade and storage of value, with Bitcoin emerging as a frontrunner. Alden suggests a long-term transition in the global financial system, indicating a shift towards neutral assets like gold and Bitcoin.
news.headline: US dollar weakening as part of long-term transition in financial system. Bitcoin and gold poised to benefit. DXY drops 11% in 2025, at April 2022 levels. Bitcoin may rally amid dollar weakening trend. Sovereign entities increasing Bitcoin exposure. Countries moving away from dollar in trade deals. Euro gaining against dollar amid ECB rate cuts. De-dollarization becoming reality with Bitcoin as a major contender.
♥️ if you agree that Reminiscences of a Stock Operator is still one of the greatest finance books ever written.

news.summary: Solar Alliance Energy Inc., a prominent solar energy solutions provider, based in Toronto and Knoxville, TN, disclosed on May 7, 2025, that the submission of its audited annual financial statements, management’s discussion and analysis, and related CEO and CFO certifications for the fiscal year ending on December 31, 2024, has encountered delays extending past the deadline of April 30, 2025. This failure to make the Required Filings, as stipulated by National Instrument 51-102, which governs Continuous Disclosure Obligations, will likely lead to Solar Alliance being marked in default. Consequently, the company foresees the halting of trading on the TSX Venture Exchange and the issuance of a cease trade order by the appropriate Canadian Securities Administrators. Solar Alliance trades under the ticker symbol TSX-V: SOLR.
news.headline: Solar Alliance Energy Inc. delays annual financial disclosure filing, faces trading halt due to missing deadline.

news.summary: Houston-based DXP Enterprises, Inc. (NASDAQ: DXPE) reported its financial results for the first quarter ending March 31, 2025. In comparison to the same period in 2024 and the last quarter of 2024, the company saw a notable increase in sales by 15.5%. Additional financial details and non-GAAP measures reconciliation can be accessed in the provided press release.
news.headline: DXP Enterprises, Inc. reports 15.5% increase in sales in Q1 2025 compared to Q1 2024. $DXPE via @businesswire

news.summary: Hyundai is leading Korea's pioneering welding humanoid project in collaboration with top AI and robotics firms. HD Hyundai Robotics and HD Korea Shipbuilding & Offshore Engineering have signed an MOU with Persona AI and Vazil to develop humanoids specifically for welding in shipyards. This initiative marks a significant step forward in harnessing advanced technology for specialized industrial tasks.
news.headline: HD Hyundai partners with Persona AI for humanoid welding robots in shipbuilding automation, aimed at enhancing efficiency.

news.summary: Pan American Silver Corp. disclosed their unaudited results for the quarter ending March 31, 2025. The company reported a successful beginning to the year, achieving a record $250.8 million in mine operating earnings and $112.6 million in free cash flow. The President and Chief Executive, Michael Steinmann, highlighted the positive outcomes driven by lower costs and increased metal prices. Pan American's (NYSE: PAAS) (TSX: PAAS) strong performance in Q1 2025 showcases their ability to capitalize on favorable market conditions and deliver robust financial results.
news.headline: Pan American Silver Corp reports record $250.8M in mine operating earnings and $112.6M in free cash flow for Q1 2025.

news.summary: iA Financial Corporation Inc. reported strong financial results for the first quarter of 2025. Their Core EPS was $2.91, a 19% increase compared to the same period in 2024. The company recorded $186 million in net income attributed to common shareholders, with a solvency ratio of 132%. They reported $5.8 billion in sales in Canada and the U.S., with assets totaling over $264 billion. The company's book value per common share reached $74.62, up 2% over 3 months. The President and CEO highlighted continued momentum and growth across all business units, showing progress towards their financial targets set for 2027. The company's quarterly financial performance by business segment also showcased positive trends, including higher core earnings in Insurance, Canada, Wealth Management, and US Operations. Overall, iA Financial Group demonstrated profitability and stability, positioning them well for sustained growth in the future.
news.headline: iA Financial Group reports strong Q1 results: Core EPS of $2.91 (+19% YoY), $5.8B in premiums, assets over $264B, solvency ratio of 132%.

news.summary: On May 7, 2025, in Washington, Acting NASA Administrator Janet Petro released a statement in response to President Donald Trump nominating Matt Anderson to be the agency's deputy administrator. Anderson, a retired United States Air Force colonel, has been selected for the role. This nomination comes as part of the administration’s efforts to strengthen the agency's leadership team.
news.headline: NASA Acting Administrator Janet Petro comments on President Trump nominating Matt Anderson as Deputy Administrator.

news.summary: In San Jose, California, Western Digital (Nasdaq: WDC) revealed the appointment of Kris Sennesael as their new Chief Financial Officer (CFO), starting May 12, 2025. Sennesael, boasting over 25 years in finance and general management within the semiconductor and technology sectors, previously held the CFO position at Skyworks Solutions. Western Digital expressed enthusiasm about Sennesael's leadership and global market expertise, emphasizing the value he brings to the company.
news.headline: Western Digital appoints Kris Sennesael as CFO starting May 12, 2025. Sennesael has 25+ years of experience in finance.

news.summary: Former SafeMoon CEO Braden John Karony professed his innocence as his criminal trial began in New York, stating he did not commit fraud in response to media coverage. Charged in 2023 were Karony, SafeMoon creator Kyle Nagy, and ex-CTO Thomas Smith for allegedly diverting and misappropriating millions of dollars' worth of the platform's SFM token. Karony implied Nagy was involved, who reportedly fled to Russia after charges. Trial began with Smith testifying against Karony and is expected to continue until May 26. Karony, free on $3 million bond, pleaded not guilty to securities fraud conspiracy, wire fraud conspiracy, and money laundering conspiracy. The case was filed in November 2023 under Trump appointee Joseph Nocella as interim US Attorney. Karony's trial, though less scrutinized than other crypto cases, may involve political factors due to Trump's alignment with the crypto industry. In a separate case, former Celsius CEO Alex Mashinsky faces sentencing on May 8, having pleaded guilty to two felony charges. Prosecutors seek a 20-year sentence for Mashinsky.
news.headline: SafeMoon's ex-CEO asserts innocence in court trial; accused of misappropriating millions. Trial ongoing, trial expected till May 26.

news.summary: Nutrien Ltd. (TSX and NYSE: NTR) from Saskatchewan declared a quarterly dividend of US$0.545 per share today. This dividend is set to be paid on July 18, 2025, to shareholders of record by June 30, 2025. Both registered shareholders in Canada as per Nutrien's register, and beneficial holders using brokers or intermediaries participating in the immediate payment system, are entitled to receive the dividend.
news.headline: Nutrien declares dividend of $0.545/share payable on July 18, 2025 to shareholders of record on June 30, 2025.

news.summary: Osisko Gold Royalties Ltd (OR: TSX & NYSE) based in Montreal, has announced that its Board of Directors approved a 20% increase in the second quarter 2025 dividend for shareholders. The dividend of US$0.055 per common share, based on the foreign exchange rate (C$/US$) during the first quarter dividend declaration, marks an upward trend. Shareholders of record as of June 30, 2025, will receive the dividend on July 15, 2025. This dividend is classified as an "eligible dividend" under the Income Tax Act (Canada).
news.headline: Osisko Gold Royalties Ltd increases Q2 2025 dividend by 20% to US$0.055 per share, payable on July 15, 2025 to shareholders.

news.summary: Great-West Lifeco Inc. announced its Q1 2025 financial results, showcasing strong performance with base earnings of $1.0 billion, up 5% from Q1 2024, and net earnings from continuing operations of $860 million, down 17% from the previous year. The company reported a base ROE of 17.2%, ROE from continuing operations of 15.6%, LICAT ratio of 130%, and Lifeco cash of $2.5 billion. Moreover, book value per share increased by 12% year over year to $27.61. Great-West Lifeco repurchased 2.1 million shares in Q1 2025 and plans to repurchase $500 million worth of shares in 2025. The company operates under brands like Canada Life, Empower, and Irish Life, serving over 40 million customer relationships in Canada, the US, and Europe. Great-West Lifeco's total client assets exceed $3 trillion as of March 31, 2025.
news.headline: Great-West Lifeco Inc. reported strong Q1 2025 results, with base earnings at $1.0 billion, 15.6% ROE from continuing operations, $2.5 billion in Lifeco cash, and repurchased 2.1 million shares worth $111 million.

news.summary: Standard Amusements, previous operator of Rye Playland, issued a rebuttal to Westchester County's claims regarding the delayed opening of the amusement park for the 2025 season. The company highlighted that it had given the county sufficient time to prepare for the season, but the county failed to utilize the months provided. This response comes amidst the county's attempts to shift blame for the delayed opening. Standard Amusements emphasized the facts, stating that the county's misrepresentations are unfounded.
news.headline: Standard Amusements responds to Westchester County's claims on Playland transition for 2025 season, highlighting county's failures in preparation.

news.summary: In the first quarter of 2024, the company reported a significant improvement in financial performance, with a net income of $27.2 million generated from a total revenue of $73.1 million. This marks a notable turnaround from the same period in the previous year, where a net loss of $6.1 million was incurred from $43.7 million in revenue. Additionally, the company successfully retired its higher-interest rate debt, specifically the 8.25% Notes, by paying a redemption price totaling $74.3 million, inclusive of any accrued and unpaid interest. These financial actions demonstrate a positive trajectory for the company's fiscal health and strategic management.
news.headline: Centrus reports Q1 2025 net income of $27.2M on $73.1M revenue, improved from $6.1M loss and $43.7M revenue in Q1 2024.

news.summary: Operating activities yielded strong cash flows of $46.1 million. This highlights the robust financial performance and effective management of resources within the company.
news.headline: Osisko Q1 2025: Operating activities yield $46.1 million cash flows, signaling strong financial performance.
Western Digital appoints Kris Sennesael Chief Financial Officer

news.summary: Nutrien Ltd. revealed its Q1 2025 financial report today, posting a net profit of $19 million, translating to diluted net earnings per share of $0.02. The adjusted EBITDA for the same period stood at $0.9 billion, with adjusted net earnings per share recorded at $0.11. The company witnessed robust potash sales volumes in the first quarter, along with heightened ammonia operating rates, setting up its retail network for an anticipated successful spring planting season in North America.
news.headline: Nutrien Ltd. reports Q1 2025 results: net earnings $19M, $0.9B adjusted EBITDA, $0.11 adjusted EPS, strong sales volumes.

news.summary: In Toronto, XTM Inc. (QB: XTMIF / CSE: PAID / FSE: 7XT), a fintech company specializing in automated tip calculations, instant payouts, and earned wage access, has released its audited financial statements and management’s discussion and analysis for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2024. The company announced this after signing an agreement on April 25, 2025.
news.headline: XTM Inc. announces 2024 financial results, files audited statements. Important figures include QB: XTMIF, CSE: PAID, FSE: 7XT.

news.summary: Plaza Retail REIT reported its financial results for the quarter ending March 31, 2025. They acquired full ownership of Tacoma Plaza, converting 40,000 sq ft into a grocery store for a $1 million incremental NOI. Another grocery store deal and construction on excess land are expected to add $700,000 in NOI. Selected IFRS financial results showed a 5.3% increase in revenue to $31,137, NOI of $18.3 million, and a net profit of $9.3 million. Selected non-IFRS results included FFO of $9.8 million, AFFO of $8.3 million, and a same-asset NOI increase of 1.5%. The company's strategy of optimize, intensify, and consolidate has shown positive results. Explanations for the financial changes were provided in the article.
news.headline: Plaza Retail REIT had strong Q1 2025 results: $31.1M revenues, $18.3M NOI, $9.3M profit. Focused strategy driving growth.

news.summary: Flutter Entertainment, a leading online sports betting and iGaming operator, reported their Q1 results and updated their 2025 guidance. In Q1 2025, they saw an 8% increase in average monthly players to 14,880, generating $3.67 billion in revenue and a net income of $335 million. Adjusted EBITDA was $616 million. The US business revenue grew by 18%, reaching $1.67 billion, with sportsbook revenue up 15% and iGaming up 32%. International revenue was stable with Southern Europe and Africa leading in growth. Flutter anticipates a 22% increase in 2025 revenue to $17.08 billion, led by the US business, with adjusted EBITDA expected to reach $3.18 billion. CEO Peter Jackson highlighted growth in the US and international markets, strategic priorities, and shareholder value creation. The company maintains a strong capital structure, targeting a leverage ratio of 2.0-2.5x, and plans to return up to $1 billion cash to shareholders in 2025. For more details, the full article can be accessed on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Flutter Entertainment, leading online betting and iGaming operator, reports Q1 2025 results: Revenue $3.67B (+8%), Net income $335M (+289%), Adjusted EBITDA $616M (+20%). CEO optimistic about US business performance and strategic priorities. Full year 2025 guidance updated to $17.08B revenue and $3.18B adjusted EBITDA.

news.summary: Beyond Meat Inc (NASDAQ: BYND) released its first-quarter financial results after market close, with Q1 revenue totaling $68.73 million, falling short of the estimated $75.01 million. The Q1 adjusted loss was 67 cents per share, higher than the estimated 48 cents. Total revenue saw a 9.1% decrease compared to last year, attributed to legal expenses, increased provisions for inventory items, and pausing operations in China. The company reported challenges as the first quarter of 2025 unfolded. For more details, the full story can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Beyond Meat reported Q1 Revenue of $68.73M, down 9.1% YoY. Adjusted Loss: 67 cents (vs. est. 48 cents). Shares slide.
